However, achieving reliable DFER performance under real\u2010world conditions remains challenging due to factors such as occlusion, head pose variation, and lighting inconsistencies. This paper proposes a robust and efficient DFER framework named MFII\u2010Net, which incorporates multi\u2010scale feature interaction and illumination balance to improve expression recognition in unconstrained driving environments. The architecture consists of three key modules: a Multi\u2010Scale Feature Interaction Module (MSFIM) that uses dual\u2010branch separable convolutions to extract fine\u2010grained and global facial features, a contrast\u2010boosted channel attention (CBCA) module that enhances feature discrimination by comparing global and local channel distributions, and an illumination balance module (IBM) that integrates wavelet decomposition, gamma correction, bilateral filtering, and multi\u2010scale CLAHE to reduce lighting\u2010induced variability. Experimental results on RAF\u2010DB and AffectNet datasets, including occlusion and pose\u2010specific subsets, and KMU\u2010FED datasets show that MFII\u2010Net outperforms existing state\u2010of\u2010the\u2010art FER models in accuracy and robustness while maintaining high computational efficiency suitable for real\u2010time driver monitoring applications.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.1049\/ipr2.70376","type":"journal-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,4,24]],"date-time":"2026-04-24T15:12:03Z","timestamp":1777043523000},"update-policy":"https:\/\/doi.org\/10.1002\/crossmark_policy","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["Robust
